content:
The user wants me to extract the content of the provided image and convert it into a JSON format. Here's a breakdown of the steps I'll take: 1. **Identify the main sections:** I'll look for headings, tables, and text blocks. 2. **Extract text:** I'll transcribe the text from each section. 3. **Format as JSON:** I'll structure the extracted data into a list of JSON objects, each representing a distinct part of the document (e.g., a heading, a table, a paragraph). **Step 1 & 2: Identifying and Extracting Content** * **Header:** "Nexperia", "PMEG3010EB" * **Sub-header:** "30 V, 1 A very low VF Schottky barrier rectifier" * **Main Heading:** "6. Ordering information" * **Table 3:** "Table 3. Ordering information" * Columns: Type number, Package (Name, Description, Version) * Row: PMEG3010EB, SC-79, plastic, surface-mounted package; 2 leads; 1.2 mm x 0.8 mm x 0.6 mm body, SOD523 * **Main Heading:** "7. Marking" * **Table 4:** "Table 4. Marking codes" * Columns: Type number, Marking code * Row: PMEG3010EB, KA * **Main Heading:** "8. Limiting values" * **Table 5:** "Table 5. Limiting values" * Text: "In accordance with the Absolute Maximum Rating System (IEC 60134)." * Columns: Symbol, Parameter, Conditions, (empty), Min, Max, Unit * Rows: * $V_R$, reverse voltage, (empty), (empty), -, 30, V * $I_F$, forward current, $T_{sp} \le 55\ ^\circ C$, (empty), -, 1, A * $I_{FRM}$, repetitive peak forward current, $t_p \le 1$ ms; $\delta \le 0.25$, (empty), -, 3, A * $I_{FSM}$, non-repetitive peak forward current, $t_p = 8$ ms; square wave; $T_{j(init)} = 25\ ^\circ C$, (empty), -, 5, A * $P_{tot}$, total power dissipation, $T_{amb} \le 25\ ^\circ C$, [1], -, 310, mW * $T_j$, junction temperature, (empty), (empty), -, 150, $^\circ C$ * $T_{amb}$, ambient temperature, (empty), (empty), -65, 150, $^\circ C$ * $T_{stg}$, storage temperature, (empty), (empty), -65, 150, $^\circ C$ * **Footnote:** "[1] Device mounted on an FR4 PCB, single-sided copper, tin-plated and standard footprint." * **Main Heading:** "9. Thermal characteristics" * **Table 6:** "Table 6. Thermal characteristics" * Columns: Symbol, Parameter, Conditions, (empty), Min, Typ, Max, Unit * Rows: * $R_{th(j-a)}$, thermal resistance from junction to ambient, in free air, [1] [2], -, -, 400, K/W * $R_{th(j-sp)}$, thermal resistance from junction to solder point, (empty), [3], -, -, 75, K/W * **Footnotes:** * [1] For Schottky barrier diodes thermal runaway has to be considered, as in some applications the reverse power losses $P_R$ are a significant part of the total power losses. * [2] Device mounted on an FR4 PCB, single-sided copper, tin-plated and standard footprint. * [3] Soldering point of cathode tab. **Step 3: Formatting as JSON** I will now construct the JSON output based on the extracted information. ```json [{"bbox": [80, 39, 171, 56], "category": "header", "text": "Nexperia"}, {"bbox": [736, 39, 917, 59], "category": "header", "text": "PMEG3010EB"}, {"bbox": [545, 67, 918, 83], "category": "header", "text": "30 V, 1 A very low VF Schottky barrier rectifier"}, {"bbox": [80, 105, 354, 124], "category": "title", "text": "6. Ordering information"}, {"bbox": [81, 139, 296, 152], "category": "table_caption", "text": "Table 3. Ordering information"}, {"bbox": [81, 152, 915, 220], "category": "table", "text": "
